title: Kilwa District Council (Community Health Fund) By-Laws, 2003
title_en: Kilwa District Council (Community Health Fund) By-Laws, 2003

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the Kilwa District Council (Community Health Fund) By-Laws, 2003.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These By-laws shall apply throughout the area of jurisdiction of Kilwa District Council .

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these By-laws unless the context otherwise requires— " Board " means a Council Health Service Board established in accordance with the provision of section 86A of the Act; [Act. No.7. of 1982] " card " meads a community health fund card; " Council " means the Kilwa Council; " contribution " means an annual voluntary contribution to the Fund; " District Medical Officer " means a medical officer in-charge of Kilwa District Council Health Services; " health care facility " means government or private health care facility and includes any other facility established or organized for purposes of delivering health services; " household " means— (a) a mother, father and children under the age of eighteen years; or (b) a member who has attained the age of eighteen years or more with or without children under the age of eighteen years or. (c) a group of five students of an institution ; " institution " means a registered school, college; or any other body as may be approved by the Board ; " member " means a contributing household to the Community Health Fund; " user fee " means a fee charged by a health care facility for services rendered.

provisions.title: Establishment of Community Health Fund
provisions.content: (1) There shall be a Community Health Fund which is a voluntary community based financing scheme whereby households pay contributions to finance part of their basic health care services to complement the Government health care financing efforts. The contributions are meant to improve the existing health services. (2) The Fund shall be managed by the Board in accordance with the provisions of these By-laws.

provisions.title: Objectives of the Fund
provisions.content: The objectives of the Fund shall be— (a) to mobilize financial resources from the community for provision of health care services to its members; (b) to provide quality and affordable health care services through sustainable financial mechanism; and (c) to improve health care services management in the communities through decentralization by empowering the communities in making decisions and by contributing on matters affecting in their health.

provisions.title: Functions of the Board
provisions.content: (1) In achieving its overall objectives the Board shall have the following functions— (a) to provide district health services and monitor Community Health Fund operations and activities; (b) to design an annual health plan for approval by the Council .

provisions.title: Sources of revenue of the Fund
provisions.content: The sources of revenue of the Fund shall be: (a) all moneys received in respect of contributions paid by members. The annual contribution of a household agreed by Kilwa Council is five thousand shillings. (b) grants from the Council , Central Government and donors; (c) donation from individuals who reside within or outside the area of jurisdiction of the Council ; (d) user charges payable for using health facilities which are under the management of the Council . The user charges for non-contributing households is set at one thousand shillings per illness. Reattandance of same individual after seven days and more will be considered another episode of illness therefore charged one thousand shillings; (e) such levies as may be determined and assigned to the Fund by the Council from time to time; (f) any other money lawfully derived by the Fund from any other source not expressly specified in these By-laws.

provisions.title: Accounts of the Board
provisions.content: (1) There shall be kept proper accounts and other records in relation to the resources of the Fund. (2) The Board shall open a separate bank account for the purposes of maintaining the revenues of the Fund from which moneys shall be deposited and drawn. (3) The signatories to the account of the Fund shall be appointed by the Board and shall constitute of category "A" and "B" and in no case moneys may be drawn from the Fund account without a signature from each of the two categories. (4) The signatories of the Fund shall be:— (a) the chairperson of the Board and one member of the Board who shall be in category "A". (b) the secretary of the Board who is a District Medical Officer and the Council Treasurer who shall be in category "B". (5) In each financial year of the Council , the Board shall submit before the Council a statement of its accounts in such form and detail as the Council may direct. (6) The accounts of the Fund shall be audited by auditors appointed in accordance with existing regulations governing auditing of the Council accounts.

provisions.title: Reports of the Fund
provisions.content: The Board shall as soon as practicable after the end of each financial year of the Council prepare a full report on the performance by it of its functions during the financial year.

provisions.title: Planning and budgeting
provisions.content: (1) Two months before the start of the Council ’s financial year the Board shall prepare a detailed plan and budget showing the amount expected to be received and disbursed to finance the delivery of health services for such development activities for the better provision of health services within the area of jurisdiction of the Council . (2) The Board may whenever circumstances so require, prepare a supplementary plan and budget. (3) The plan and budget of the Fund prepared in accordance with the provisions of paragraph (1) and (2) of this by-law shall be submitted to the Council for approval.

provisions.title: Composition of the Board
provisions.content: There shall be a Board which shall be composed of: (a) four community service users of the health facility of whom at least two shall be female; (b) one representative, each from a non-profit voluntary agency and a private for profit health care facility , appointed by the Council from amongst health care facilities which have entered into a Service Agreement with the Board ; (c) the Chairman of the Council Social Services Committee; (d) the Council Planning Officer; (e) the District Medical Officer who shall be the Secretary to the Board ; (f) one representative from the hospital; and (g) one representative from the Regional Health Management Team.

provisions.title: Qualifications of members of Board
provisions.content: A person shall be qualified to be a member under paragraph (a) of By-law 11 if— (a) he is a citizen of Tanzania; (b) he is of the age between 25 to 70 years; (c) he has at least completed secondary school education from a registered school; (d) has experience and proven abilities in social services; and (e) has leadership qualities.

provisions.title: Tenure of office of members
provisions.content: (1) A member of the Board shall hold office for a period of three years from the date of his nomination and may be re-elected for another one term of three years only. (2) If any vacancy occurs in the membership of the Board by reason of death, resignation, permanent incapacity or for any other reason, another person may be nominated to fill the vacancy and a person so nominated shall hold office for the un-expired residue period of office of the member in whose place he is nominated.

provisions.title: Management of the Fund
provisions.content: (1) The management and administration of the Fund shall vest— (a) at district level; to the Council , through the Council Health Service Board ; (b) at ward level; to the Ward Development Committee, through the Ward Health Committee; (c) at village level; to the Village Council , through the Village Social Services Committee. (2) The Fund shall be under the overall management of the Board which shall be responsible for— (a) coordinating the activities of the Fund; (b) monitoring, controlling and directing the management of the resources of the Fund; (c) ensuring that the Fund is effectively and efficiently used and managed.

provisions.title: Application of resources of the Fund
provisions.content: The Board shall apply the resources of the Fund for the payment of the services rendered, and shall be limited to the fulfillment of the objectives of the Fund and in so doing, be governed by principle of efficiency, effectiveness, economy and equity.

provisions.title: Membership and issuing of card
provisions.content: (1) Each member of the Fund shall be issued with a membership card upon paying the annual contribution , which shall be proposed by the Ward Health Committee to the Council through the Board and approved on an annual basis by the Council . (2) Subject to the provisions of the Community Health Fund Act, 2001, each exempted person, shall be issued with a membership card . [ Act No. 1 of 2001 ]

provisions.title: Provision of health services
provisions.content: (1) Each member shall be entitled to receive prepaid medical services from a pre-selected health care facility within the area of jurisdiction of the Council on the following basis. (a) medical service to himself or to herself and his or her spouse and children of age of minority; (b) making proposals on the health care facility of his or her choice which shall be taken into account by the Board ; (c) Involvement in the overall planning and implementation of health care services delivery in the area of jurisdiction of the Council .

provisions.title: Transfer of assets and liabilities
provisions.content: Notwistanding the repeal of the Kilwa District Council CHF Bylaws, under by-law 19, all assets and liabilities which were previously vested in the CHF District Health Board under the repealed By-laws shall be deemed to be transferred to the Board .
